code.code:
# Parse map to find exact coordinates of the down-stairs '>'
map_lines = obs.split('--- MAP ---')[1].split('--- STATUS ---')[0].strip().split('\n')
stairs_pos = None
for y, line in enumerate(map_lines):
    x = line.find('>')
    if x != -1:
        stairs_pos = (x, y)
        print(f"Found stairs at ({x}, {y})")
        break

if stairs_pos:
    # Calculate path to stairs and move towards it
    target_x, target_y = stairs_pos
    player_x, player_y = 5, 17
    
    # Determine direction to move
    if target_y < player_y:
        # Move north (up on screen)
        direction = 'N' if target_x == player_x else ('NE' if target_x > player_x else 'NW')
    elif target_y > player_y:
        # Move south (down on screen)
        direction = 'S' if target_x == player_x else ('SE' if target_x > player_x else 'SW')
    else:
        # Same row, move horizontally
        direction = 'E' if target_x > player_x else 'W'
    
    print(f"Moving {direction} towards stairs")
    nh.move(direction)
else:
    print("Stairs not found, searching current area")
    nh.search()

# Let me check current map and find stairs
map_lines = []
capturing = False
for line in history.splitlines():
    if '--- MAP ---' in line:
        capturing = True
        continue
    if capturing and line.strip() == '':
        break
    if capturing:
        map_lines.append(line)

# Find the row with > in current obs
for i, line in enumerate(map_lines):
    if '>' in line:
        print(f"Row {i}: {repr(line)}")
        # Find position of > character
        idx = line.index('>')
        print(f"Stairs at column {idx}")

# From status, pos=(5,17). Looking at map, > is west of @

print(f"\nCurrent position: (5,17)")
print("Moving west toward stairs")
nh.move('W')
